message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR integer canonical-encoding length-boundary fuzz target (resolves lwzfk)

to_canonical_cbor MUST emit integers in shortest-form per RFC 8949
§4.2.1. The byte-length boundary table is:
  [0,23]            → 1 byte (head 0x00..0x17 = value)
  [24,255]          → 2 bytes (head 0x18 + 1 byte)
  [256,65535]       → 3 bytes (head 0x19 + 2 bytes BE)
  [65536,u32::MAX]  → 5 bytes (head 0x1a + 4 bytes BE)
  > u32::MAX        → 9 bytes (head 0x1b + 8 bytes BE)

Negative integers use head bits 0x20-0x3b (Major Type 1 = NegInt).

Existing cbor_float_encoding_edges (1y12u) covers Float↔Integer type
non-aliasing but NOT the integer length-boundary table. A regression
that emitted longer-than-shortest forms would let ObjectIds drift
across implementations and break the canonical-encoding gate in
CanonicalSerializer::deserialize.

Four properties: u64 length boundary (1/2/3/5/9), i64 NegInt length
boundary, ciborium round-trip on encoded bytes (decoded value matches
original).

Once-gated regression anchors verify exact encodings for boundary
values:
- u64: {0,23,24,255,256,65535,65536,u32::MAX,u32::MAX+1} → exact bytes
- i64: {-1,-24,-25,-256,-257,-65536,-65537} → exact bytes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
